title: medicago asiatica subsp. turkestanica
intro: Medicago asiatica subsp. turkestanica, also known as Turkestan clover, is a subspecies of the annual legume plant Medicago asiatica, native to Central Asia and often grown as a forage crop. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Medicago asiatica subsp. turkestanica include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and even exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and skin problems.
